name: blackboard
summary: Multiple agents read and write to a shared 'blackboard' file, evolving a solution collaboratively. Coordinator synthesizes at the end.
when_to_use: Multi-agent review where specialists need to see each other's findings (vs pure parallelization where they don't).

layers.skill: skills/agent-teams documents blackboard as a coordination protocol option.
layers.hook: Optional: PostToolUse Write hook that notifies other agents when the blackboard file changes.
layers.agent: agents/council-coordinator implements blackboard protocol. Specialists write to .claude/reviews/{specialist}.md; coordinator synthesizes VERDICT.md.

cost: ~3–6× normal. Expensive because each agent reads the full blackboard on each pass.
anti_patterns: Blackboard with fewer than 3 agents — overhead not justified.
No write discipline on blackboard — agents overwrite each other's notes.
Coordinator also writes to blackboard during synthesis — confuses the state model.
